content.problemStatement:
You have NNN stones in a row, numbered from 111 to NNN. You also have KKK different colors with you.

We know that the number of different ways of coloring these NNN stones with these KKK colors is KNK^NKN. But you now add an extra condition:


If ∣j−i∣<K|j - i| < K∣j−i∣<K, then Stone iii and Stone jjj cannot have the same color.
In other words, you cannot color two stones with the same color if they are <K\lt K<K distance apart.

Find the number of different ways of coloring the stones with this extra condition. Since the answer might be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of stones and the number of colors that you have, respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of different ways of coloring, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
If there are KKK colors, we will denote the colors as C1,C2,…,CKC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_KC1​,C2​,…,CK​.
Testcase 1: N=1N = 1N=1, and K=1K = 1K=1.
So we need to find the number of ways of coloring 111 stone, using the colors {C1}\{C_1\}{C1​}, and ensuring that no two stones which are at a distance of <1< 1<1 apart have the same color.
The only way to do so is by coloring Stone 111 with C1C_1C1​. So the answer is 111.
Testcase 2: N=1N = 1N=1, and K=2K = 2K=2.
So we need to find the number of ways of coloring 111 stone, using the colors {C1,C2}\{C_1, C_2\}{C1​,C2​}, and ensuring that no two stones which are at a distance of <2< 2<2 apart have the same color.
The two ways to do so are by coloring Stone 111 with either C1C_1C1​ or C2C_2C2​. So the answer is 222.
Testcase 3: N=2N = 2N=2, and K=1K = 1K=1.
So we need to find the number of ways of coloring 222 stones, using the colors {C1}\{C_1\}{C1​}, and ensuring that no two stones which are at a distance of <1< 1<1 apart have the same color.
The only way to do so is by coloring both Stones with C1C_1C1​. So the answer is 111.
Testcase 4: N=2N = 2N=2, and K=2K = 2K=2.
So we need to find the number of ways of coloring 222 stones, using the colors {C1,C2}\{C_1, C_2\}{C1​,C2​}, and ensuring that no two stones which are at a distance of <2< 2<2 apart have the same color.
The two ways to do so are:

coloring Stone 111 with C1C_1C1​ and Stone 222 with C2C_2C2​
coloring Stone 111 with C2C_2C2​ and Stone 222 with C1C_1C1​

Note that you cannot color both Stones with the same color, since 2−1<K2 - 1 < K2−1<K.
So, the answer is 222.
